_comment: Phrases the tripwire checker bans from generated copy. Each entry is matched against every slide body and the caption. A single match fails the run. Replace these placeholder categories with categories that fit your domain.

categories.description: Replace this category. Common examples: unattributed health claims, financial guarantees, age-targeted marketing language, regulated-product claims (alcohol, supplements, medical devices).
categories.rationale: Why this category exists — what real-world risk you're avoiding. The review UI shows this rationale when a tripwire fires, so write it for your future self.

categories.description: A second category, demonstrating how to group related bans.
categories.rationale: Different rationale for a different risk.

_match_types_comment: Each phrase has a 'match' field: 'literal' for exact substring (case-insensitive by default), or 'regex' for pattern matching with optional 'flags'. The checker is intentionally aggressive — false positives are cheap, false negatives are expensive.
_seeding_comment: When seeding this file, write the rationale BEFORE the phrases. If you can't articulate why a phrase is banned, you'll struggle to defend the ban later, and you'll be tempted to remove it the first time it fires on copy you wanted to ship.
